subject: [PATCH v2 21/21] docs: fuse: document extended passthrough (FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO)

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
The reviewer pointed out that the documentation incorrectly states that files opened with FOPEN_DIRECT_IO deny entry into both caching and passthrough IOMODE, when in fact they only deny entry into caching.

Reviewer noted that the requirement for FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH when opening files in caching mode without doing IO passthrough could be perplexing to users, and suggested either documenting this limitation or relaxing the restriction.
reviews.raw_body:
More accurately, no open files in caching or passthough.
files open with FOPEN_DIRECT_IO may exist and they
do not deny entering either caching or passthrough iomode.

---

I had not considered this.
Setting OP_GETATTR/SETATTR in LOOKUP without any OP_RW bits
and then requiring that the server open files with FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH
with the backing_id sounds strange -
I understand why it was done this way, but for users this could be perplexing.

The thing is that if the server is only doing GETATTR passthrough there is not
really a reason to deny open file in caching mode (I think?).

The limitation that requires FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH is encoded in
fuse_file_cached_io_open()
    fuse_is_io_cache_wait()
        !fuse_inode_backing()

We have two options.

Either we decide that server passing through GETATTR and opening
files in caching mode is not interesting to support (for now) and then just
maybe we need to better document the reason that server needs to
use FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH despite not doing io passthrough.

Or we relax the limitation w.r.t cached io mode, something like this
(completely untested most likely broken based or earlier review
suggestion regarding fuse_passthrough_op):

Thanks,
Amir.

diff --git a/fs/fuse/iomode.c b/fs/fuse/iomode.c
index c5f3e2201c6ab..7c33e7a974f6c 100644
--- a/fs/fuse/iomode.c
+++ b/fs/fuse/iomode.c
@@ -20,7 +20,8 @@ static inline bool fuse_is_io_cache_wait(struct inode *inode)
        struct fuse_inode *fi = get_fuse_inode(inode);

        return S_ISREG(inode->i_mode) &&
-               READ_ONCE(fi->iocachectr) < 0 && !fuse_inode_backing(fi);
+               READ_ONCE(fi->iocachectr) < 0 &&
+               !fuse_passthrough_op(inode, FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_RW_OPS);
 }

 /*
@@ -54,7 +55,7 @@ int fuse_file_cached_io_open(struct inode *inode,
struct fuse_file *ff)
         * Check if inode entered passthrough io mode while waiting for parallel
         * dio write completion.
         */
-       if (fuse_inode_backing(fi)) {
+       if (fuse_passthrough_op(inode, FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_RW_OPS)) {
                clear_bit(FUSE_I_CACHE_IO_MODE, &fi->state);
                spin_unlock(&fi->lock);
                return -ETXTBSY;
@@ -285,7 +286,8 @@ int fuse_file_io_open(struct file *file, struct
inode *inode)
         * which is already open for passthrough.
         */
        err = -EINVAL;
-       if (fuse_inode_backing(fi) && !(ff->open_flags & FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH))
+       if (fuse_passthrough_op(inode, FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_RW_OPS) &&
+           !(ff->open_flags & FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH))
                goto fail;

        /*

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that fuse_inode_set_passthrough() still takes the negative iocache refcount, indicating a potential issue with passthrough setup in FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO mode.
reviews.raw_body:
Obviously, this was naive and not close to enough, because
fuse_inode_set_passthrough() still takes the negative iocachectr
refcount.

Please ignore this. Supporting caching iomode and GETATTR
passthrough is left for a future exercise, if anyone is ever interested.

Thanks,
Amir.

2026-05-16.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) module, specifically about extended passthrough mode (FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO). This new feature allows for inode operations and directory operations to be passed through from the user space to the kernel, with a one-to-one mapping between fuse inode numbers and backing file inode numbers. The documentation explains how to enable this feature, including steps for negotiating the capability during FUSE_INIT, registering backing files, and handling requests in the daemon.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledges that the documentation will need to be updated once a change to inode->i_ino is merged into the fuse tree, promising an update in the first patch.

Author acknowledged that extended passthrough mode is currently not supported and agreed to leave it out of the documentation for now.
reviews.raw_body:
When Jeff's changes for u64 inode->i_ino land in the fuse tree, I'll
get rid of this and update the 1st patch accordingly.

---

Sounds good, I will continue to leave this unsupported for now.

Thanks for reviewing these patches.

2026-05-18.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) module, specifically about extended passthrough mode (FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO). This new feature allows for inode operations and directory operations to be passed through from the user space to the kernel, with a one-to-one mapping between fuse inode numbers and backing file inode numbers. The documentation explains how to enable this feature, including steps for negotiating the capability during FUSE_INIT, registering backing files, and handling requests in the daemon.
